children.name: Vanga
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Hook-billed Vanga, the namesake of the vanga family, endemic to Madagascar. It has a thick, strongly hooked bill that is distinctly crossed at the tip, an adaptation for extracting prey from tree crevices. It is a medium-sized, boldly patterned bird with a black-and-white plumage that makes it easily identifiable in the Malagasy forests.

children.name: Artamella
children.description: A monotypic genus of vanga endemic to Madagascar, containing the White-headed Vanga. It has a striking white head contrasting with black wings and tail, and a relatively slender, straight bill adapted for gleaning insects from foliage. It is a social species often seen in small groups.

children.name: Leptopterus
children.description: A monotypic genus of vanga containing the Chabert Vanga, a small, pale-eyed species endemic to Madagascar. It has a relatively slender, straight bill and is an active canopy insectivore, often joining mixed-species flocks. Its pale blue-grey plumage makes it one of the more subtly coloured vangas.

children.name: Mystacornis
children.description: A monotypic genus of terrestrial vanga endemic to Madagascar, containing Crossley's Vanga. It has a long, slender, slightly decurved bill and strong legs, adapted for a ground-foraging lifestyle in the leaf litter of the forest floor. It is one of the most secretive and poorly known members of the vanga family.

children.name: Schetba
children.description: A monotypic genus of vanga containing the Rufous Vanga, a medium-sized, sexually dimorphic species endemic to Madagascar. The male is strikingly coloured with a rufous back and head, while the female is more subdued. It has a relatively stout bill and forages actively in the forest mid-storey.

children.name: Calicalicus
children.description: A genus of small, sexually dimorphic vangas endemic to Madagascar, with males sporting bright red or chestnut patches on the wings and tail. They are among the smallest members of the vanga family and are active, acrobatic foragers in the forest canopy. Their short, stout bills are adapted for gleaning insects from foliage rather than the specialised foraging of other vangas.

children.name: Oriolia
children.description: A monotypic genus of vanga containing Bernier's Vanga, a striking black-and-white species endemic to Madagascar. It has a stout, slightly hooked bill and a distinctive white wing patch. It is a forest-dwelling insectivore that forages actively in the canopy, often in mixed-species flocks.

children.name: Xenopirostris
children.description: A genus of three medium-sized vangas endemic to Madagascar, characterised by a laterally compressed, blade-like bill that is unique among birds. This remarkable adaptation is used to peel bark and pry into crevices in search of prey. They inhabit dry forests and are among the most specialised foragers in the vanga family.

children.name: Falculea
children.description: A monotypic vanga genus containing the Sickle-billed Vanga, which possesses the most extreme bill morphology in the family: a long, slender, strongly decurved bill resembling a sickle. This highly specialised tool is used to probe deep into tree crevices and bark furrows for large insects. Endemic to Madagascar, it is the largest species in the vanga family.

children.name: Cyanolanius
children.description: A monotypic genus of strikingly coloured vanga with brilliant azure-blue upperparts that are unique among Malagasy passerines. It has a relatively slender, straight bill and is more of a canopy insectivore than other vangas. Its distribution extends beyond Madagascar to the Comoros Islands, unusual for the family, which is otherwise entirely restricted to Madagascar.

children.name: Euryceros
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the extraordinary Helmet Vanga, one of the most distinctive birds in Madagascar. It has a massive, deep, laterally compressed bill that is a vivid blue-grey and forms a prominent casque on the forehead. This remarkable adaptation is used to crush large insects and other prey. It is a large, robust vanga of eastern rainforests.

children.name: Hypositta
children.description: A monotypic genus of small vanga containing the Nuthatch Vanga, which convergently resembles a nuthatch in both morphology and behaviour. It has a short, straight bill and strong feet, and is unique among vangas for its tree-creeping foraging style, spiralling up trunks and branches in search of insects. Endemic to Madagascar.

children.name: Newtonia
children.description: A genus of small, warbler-like vangas endemic to Madagascar, with relatively slender, pointed bills and active foraging styles. They were traditionally placed in the Old World warbler family Sylviidae before molecular studies revealed their true affinities within the vanga family. They are insectivorous birds of forest and scrub habitats.

children.name: Newtonia micrura
children.description: A very small vanga with short tail, found in dry forests of western Madagascar.

children.name: Newtonia larvata
children.description: A masked newtonia of the Comoro Islands.

children.name: Pseudocossyphus
children.description: Small vangas formerly placed in the thrush family.

children.name: Tylas
children.description: A monotypic genus of vanga with striking black-and-white plumage.

children.name: Tylas albicollis
children.description: A striking black-and-white vanga from eastern Madagascar.

children.name: Oryzoborus
children.description: A genus of seed-eating birds formerly placed in the tanager family.

children.name: Tylas
children.description: A monotypic genus of vanga.

children.name: Tylas albicollis
children.description: A striking black-and-white vanga from eastern Madagascar.

children.name: Pseudocossyphus
children.description: Small vangas from Madagascar.

children.description: Small vangas from Madagascar.

children.name: Tylas
children.description: A monotypic genus of vanga.

children.description: A striking black-and-white vanga from eastern Madagascar.
